symptomSummary: Evolve command stops incorporating recent thumbs-down feedback after a revision reaches ~30 invocations, instead generating changes based on stale feedback from weeks earlier.
rootCauseHypothesis: The repository query fetching feedback for evolution likely has a LIMIT clause (around 30) without proper ORDER BY timestamp DESC, causing it to return the oldest 30 invocations instead of the most recent 30.
fixHypothesis: Add or correct the ORDER BY clause in the feedback query to sort by timestamp descending before applying any LIMIT, ensuring recent feedback is prioritized over old feedback.

primaryLocation.rationale: This file contains SQL query logic for the repository layer and was recently modified. The ~30 invocation threshold strongly suggests a LIMIT clause in a feedback-fetching query without proper time-based ordering. This is where feedback would be filtered and sorted before being passed to the evolution service.

candidateLocations.rationale: Orchestrates the evolution process and calls the repository to fetch feedback. May contain logic that aggregates or weights feedback incorrectly, though less likely to have the threshold behavior than a SQL query.
The API endpoint for evolve operations. Small file (31 lines) so likely just routes to the evolution service, but could contain query parameters that affect feedback retrieval.
The agent that processes feedback to enhance prompts. Could contain flawed feedback weighting logic, though the threshold symptom points more to data retrieval than processing.
